rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant E.S. Windows Llc To Respond To Request For Production Of Documents And To Produce Responsive Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against Es Windows Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT SWINERTON BUILDERS' Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant E.S. Windows Llc To Respond To Request For Production Of Documents And To Produce Responsive Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against Es Windows Llc Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As the parties had an agreement to e-serve discovery and it is conceded that at least one of the attorneys for the responding parties received notice although the proof of service incorrectly states Mr. Van Dyke was served by email, the motion to compel is granted, however, responding party shall be able to assert objections. Responses shall be filed and documents produced to the extent available on or before January 3, 2020. The discovery motion deadline with respect to motions regarding the response will not apply so as to allow moving party to bring a further motion to compel if necessary prior to trial. Sanctions awarded to Swinerton in the amount of $1,860. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PRIATKO VS. GORDON JOSEPH PRIATKO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside, Open And Or Vacate Judgment Of Dismissal Of Action With Prejudice, After Sustaining Of Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend And Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Complaint, If Entered
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 1, (Part 1 of 2 for Tentative Ruling) PLAINTIFF JOHN PRIATKO's Motion To Set Aside, Open And Or Vacate Judgment Of Dismissal Of Action With Prejudice, After Sustaining Of Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend And Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Complaint, If Entered. Plaintiff John Priatko's motion to set aside, open, and/or vacate judgment of dismissal of action with prejudice is DENIED. Plaintiff was properly served and had actual notice of the August 28, 2017 hearing on Defendants' demurrer, which ultimately was sustained without leave to amend. Code of Civil Procedure § 473.5, therefore, does not provide grounds to set aside the judgment. Nor is the judgment void or contain clerical mistakes to be corrected. (Code Civ. Proc., § 473(d).) Plaintiff has not made a showing of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect" sufficient for the Court to grant relief under Code of Civil Procedure § 473(b). Plaintiff argues he was in the hospital the morning of the August 28, 2017 hearing. However, Plaintiff's motion reveals he was able to file an untimely opposition to the demurrer the morning of the hearing and his hospital intake form shows he was not admitted until 8:17 p.m. (Plaintiff's Motion, Ex. D.) Furthermore, Plaintiff waited over two years after the entry of the order sustaining Defendants' demurrer without leave to amend and six months after the dismissal of the action to file this motion. "In order to qualify for relief under section 473, the moving party must act diligently in seeking relief." (Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 234.) If there is a substantial delay between discovery of the default and defendant's filing a motion for relief under § 473(b), defendant must show a reasonable excuse for the delay. (Ludka v. Memory Magnetics Int'l (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 316, 321.) Delays of three months or more routinely result in denial of relief absent satisfactory explanation for the delay. (See Stafford v. Mach (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1174, 1184; see also Minick v. City of Petaluma (2016) 3 Cal.App.5th 15, 34 ["A delay is unreasonable as a matter of law only when it exceeds three months and there is no evidence to explain the delay."].) Plaintiff offers none. (tentative ruling continued, see next entry).

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PRIATKO VS. GORDON JOSEPH PRIATKO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff John Priatko'S Motion To Set Aside, Open And Or Vacate Judgment Of Dismissal Of Action With Prejudice, After Sustaining Of Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend And Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Complaint, If Entered. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 1, Plaintiff John Priatko'S Motion To Set Aside, Open And Or Vacate Judgment Of Dismissal Of Action With Prejudice, After Sustaining Of Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend And Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Complaint, If Entered. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling). After the six-month period for statutory relief has passed, the court may still grant relief on equitable grounds, including extrinsic fraud or mistake. (Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 47.) There is no evidence of extrinsic fraud or mistake to indicate Plaintiff was prevented from filing a timely opposition to the demurrer or from appearing in Court for the hearing on demurrer. To the contrary, the record shows that the Court was very flexible and granted a continuance when Plaintiff requested moving the hearing from July 26, 2017 to August 28, 2017. Plaintiff filed a late opposition to the demurrer on the morning of the August 2017 hearing, which included the date in which he was to appear. Plaintiff contends that opposing counsel and the Court were notified prior to the hearing of the demurrer that he could not participate. Plaintiff argues the hearing should have been continued, but Plaintiff does not attach copies of the correspondence he refers to or any requests that the hearing be continued a second time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DEBBRAH RUFFIN VS. RAFAEL GUZMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders And Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2033 280 And For An Order That Defense Counsel Verify By Declaration Compliance With California Rules Of Professional Conduct 3 510
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF DEBBRAH RUFFIN'S Motion For Orders And Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2033 280 And For An Order That Defense Counsel Verify By Declaration Compliance With California Rules Of Professional Conduct 3 510 Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The request that defense counsel prepare a declaration is denied without prejudice as this was not the subject of any formal discovery request. As for the Motion regarding the Requests for Admissions, since timely responses were made that contained objections, the objections are preserved even without a verification. (See Brown & Weil, §§ 8:1360; 8:1364.) Since the verification was not provided until after the motion was filed, sanctions are warranted in the amount of $1560 as Ruffin did not prevail on all issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ABHIJIT PRASAD VS. BRAM FRIDHANDLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BRAM FRIDHANDLER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Bram Fridhandler's demurrer to plaintiff Abhijit Prasad's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action as the doctrine of res judicata or claim preclusion bars his claims. (Code Civ. Proc. § 430.10(e).) "Res judicata bars a cause of action that was or could have been litigated in a prior proceeding if: '(1) the present action is on the same cause of action as the prior proceeding; (2) the prior proceeding resulted in a final judgment on the merits; and (3) the parties in the present action or parties in privity with them were parties to the prior proceeding. [Citation.]'" (Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco v. Countrywide Financial Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 1520, 1527.) In this case, all of the elements for the application of the doctrine of claim preclusion are present. The court grants defendant's request for judicial notice per Evidence Code § 452(d). The court is allowed to take judicial notice of court records. Those records disclose that plaintiff commenced case CGC-18-566564 against defendant based on the same facts that underpin the instant action. Judge Kahn dismissed the prior action based on the doctrine of quasi-judicial immunity and judgment was subsequently entered on March 18, 2019. Plaintiff did not appeal that judgment and it is final. The judgment was on the merits as the court determined "the real or substantial grounds of action or defense as distinguished from matters of practice, procedure, jurisdiction or form." (Boccardo v. Safeway Stores, Inc. (1982) 134 Cal.App.3d 1037, 1042.) The court rejects plaintiff's argument that Judge Kahn did not consider his contractual claims. The prior complaint clearly alleges a claim for breach of contract. (CGC-18-566564 Complaint, 5.6.1 - 5.6.15.) Judge Kahn's order sustaining the demurrer did so as to "ALL CAUSES OF ACTION," which necessarily includes the breach of contract claim. If plaintiff believed that the doctrine of quasi-judicial immunity did not apply to a breach of contract claim, then it was incumbent upon plaintiff to appeal Judge Kahn's decision rather than commence repetitive litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order Compelling Jen Lu To Further Respond To Form Interrogatories And For A Req. For A Req. Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Admit as to Defendant Scott Carter is denied without prejudice. Although deemed admissions can only be used against the party to whom the requests were made, the requests for admissions are required to be served on all other parties (CCP §2033.070). Given the interrelationship of the parties and the fact that Carter has not appeared in this action and his default has not been taken, the motion is denied without prejudice. rulings.Case Title: ARMANDO REYES VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Seal Highly-Confidential Medical Records Submitted In Violation Of California Federal-Law And Req. For Hardcore Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order To Seal Highly-Confidential Medical Records Submitted In Violation Of California Federal-Law And Req. For Hardcore Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to seal is denied. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. "Unless confidentiality is required by law, court records are presumed to be open." (Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 2.550(c).) Records may be sealed only if the court "expressly finds" that "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; [] (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; [] (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; [] (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and [] (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (Rule 2.550(d).) "The burden of presenting information . . . is logically placed upon the party seeking the sealing of the documents, who is presumptively in the best position to know what disclosures will harm him and how." (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) Plaintiff fails to meet this burden, instead summarily claiming that he has an overriding interest in keeping the information from the public and that he would be prejudiced if this information was made public for anyone to see. (Plf.'s MPA 8-9.) The contents of the 12 pages at issue are medical chart notes all related to Mr. Reye's alleged ankle injury-an injury Mr. Reyes placed in controversy by bringing this personal injury action-and do not contain particularly sensitive information. Further, Plaintiff himself attached the chart notes to his demand letter to defendant, and did not object to their production by his medical providers. Finally, Plaintiff admits that sealing would "not prevent defendants from either discovering relevant information about Plaintiff Reye's medical condition . . . nor will it bar them from producing these documents in the evidence portion of the trial." (Plf.'s MPA 6.) Plaintiff has not shown that the requirements of the Rule are met. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY MA ET AL VS. PATRICK FOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses, To Compel Compliance, And To Impose Monetary Sanctions On Defendants Patrick Foy, Franklin Loffer, Np Sand And Sea Partners, Llc, Nphss, Llc, And North Point Ocean Partners, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS JEFFREY MA, RYAN GIN, MICHAEL LUU'S Motion To Compel Further Responses, To Compel Compliance, And To Impose Monetary Sanctions On Defendants Patrick Foy, Franklin Loffer, Np Sand And Sea Partners, Llc, Nphss, Llc, And North Point Ocean Partners, Llc Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel The initial Separate Statement was incomprehensible and the subsequent one does not enable the defendants to respond as it was served after the opposition was filed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the only issue seems to be whether there are text messages that have not been produced by Foy and Loffer. (See Memo of P & A. p. 6 II). The motion to strike the Foy text is denied as it is not being used for an improper purpose under Evidence Code §1152. Defendants, however, have attached declarations from each of these individuals stating that they have produced all documents and do not have any other texts. Under these circumstances, motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded to either side. rulings.Case Title: NAYELI ALVARADO ET AL VS. ERICK JAVIER PACHECHO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT VANTAGE MAINTENANCE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Vantage Maintenance's demurrer to the complaint is overruled in its entirety. Liberally construing the complaint, Plaintiffs adequately plead sufficient facts to establish an employer-employee relationship between Plaintiffs and Vantage Maintenance. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 452; Compl., 2, 5, 9, 11.) Vantage Maintenance argues the allegations use boilerplate language, but at the outset of litigation, the amount of specificity in Plaintiffs' complaint adequately gives the Defendants notice of the issues. (See, e.g., Barsegian v. Kessler & Kessler (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 446, 451.) Similarly, at the pleading stage, Plaintiffs do not have to specifically name which of the alleged joint employers were liable for the acts upon which liability is based. (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) The issue of whether Vantage Maintenance employed Plaintiffs is a factual issue not suitable for determination on demurrer. (See, e.g., Richtek USA, Inc. uPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 660 ["A demurrer is simply not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts."].) Likewise, the complaint is neither uncertain nor ambiguous. A "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond."].) Further, whether or not Vantage Maintenance employed Plaintiffs is presumptively within Vantage's knowledge. (Chen v. Berenjian (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 811, 822 [A demurrer for uncertainty will almost certainly be overruled where the facts alleged in the complaint are presumptively within the knowledge of the demurring party or ascertainable by invoking discovery procedures.]) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA GOLUNOVA ET AL VS. ANN AKHROMSTSEV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFFs VICTORIA GOLUNOVA and MARIYA RYCHKOVA's Motion For Order Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiffs' motion to appoint receiver and for a preliminary injunction is granted. Plaintiffs show that Daniel Akhromtsev has been using CHH's funds to make questionable expenditures, including monthly car payments, credit card payments, gasoline, grocery shopping, dining, food delivery, and other personal expenses; and generally that Defendants have been managing CHH without the full knowledge or consent of Plaintiffs, although CHH's operating agreement provides that it "shall be controlled by its Members, acting unanimously." Defendants summarily assert that the challenged payments constituted ordinary business expenses, but fail adequately to explain or substantiate that contention. Plaintiffs, who own 66% of CHH, are entitled to a receiver to protect their interests in that entity. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 564(b)(9).) The court intends to request the receiver, as his first order of business, to conduct an independent audit of CHH and report his findings and recommendations back to the court. The court intends by that request to attempt to minimize the expense and burden on the parties and the business stemming from the appointment of a receiver. Plaintiffs should present at the hearing a modified proposed form of order incorporating that request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars. Denied. "For many purposes a bill of particulars, when made in response to a statutory demand, is an amplification of the complaint. It makes plaintiff's demand more specific, and limits his proof to the items set out." (Edelman v. McDonnell (1899) 126 Cal.210, 213.) Defendant's request prior to litigation, prior to becoming an adverse party or there being demands in need of further specification does not constitute the demand contemplated under Code of Civ. Proc. § 454. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Exhaustion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Exhaustion. Denied. What Defendant is asserting is an affirmative defense, not proper on a motion to dismiss/demurrer. "There is no statutory authority for using a motion to dismiss as a method to challenge opposing pleadings. Under the Code, dismissal motions are expressly authorized only on specified grounds." (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-D, § 7:370.) Demurrers are similarly limited to the grounds specified in Code fo Civ. Proc. 430.10. Such an argument should be raised as an affirmative defense in D's answer, provided for in Code of Civ. Proc. § 431.30(g). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled because the loan agreement is attached as exhibit A to the complaint and therefore it can be "ascertained from the pleading" that the contract is a written contract, per § 430.10(g). The demurrers to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action are overruled because it is a well-established principle that a complaint can allege multiple causes of action, pled in the alternative. (See Mendoza v. Cont'l Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. A "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["the trial court indicated that demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond. We agree…"].) Therefore, although 13 does seem to contain a typo, when read against the complaint as a whole, the pleading is not so incomprehensible that Defendant cannot respond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. Denied. Under Code of Civ. Proc. § 413.10(b) summons on someone outside of California shall be served "as provided in this chapter or as prescribed by the law of the place where the person is served." Under § 415.40, "summons may be served on a person outside this state in any manner provided by this article." One of the ways provided for in the Code of Civ. Proc. under § 415.10 is "by personal delivery of a copy of the summons and of the complaint to the person to be served. Service of a summons in this manner is deemed complete at the time of such delivery." Defendant was personally served and thus service was complete in accordance with California law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of An Inconvenient Forum, Or In The Alternative For A Stay Of Entire Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's tion And Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of An Inconvenient Forum, Or In The Alternative For A Stay Of Entire Action. Denied. Plaintiff and Defendant's loan agreement contains a mandatory forum selection clause, selecting San Francisco County for all disputes. (Compl., Ex. A, 6.) Thus, Defendant has contracted away his right to choose the forum. (Berg v. MTC Electronics Tch. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 349, 358 ["The factors that apply generally to a forum non conveniens motion do not control in a case involving a mandatory forum selection clause."]; Cal-State Bus. Prod. & Servs. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1666, 1683 "[T]he general factors [of forum non conveniens] are pertinent only in the absence of a contractual provision, since under those circumstances neither party possesses a right to any particular forum . . . However, a party which has contracted away its right to choose its home forum . . . may be required to litigate disputes elsewhere."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction. Denied. "[S]uperior courts are courts of general subject matter jurisdiction and can adjudicate any and all cases before them." (Long v. Forty Niners Football (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 550, 556.) Thus, this court has subject matter jurisdiction even if there is also subject matter jurisdiction in federal court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. "A demurrer lies for lack of standing when the defect appears on the face of the pleading or from judicially noticeable matters." (Qualified Patients Assn. v. City of Anaheim (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 734, 752.) That Zions is not the real party in interest and thus has no standing to sue is not apparent from the face of the pleading nor from matters judicially noticeable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of Fraud In The Inducement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of Fraud In The Inducement. Denied. What Defendant is asserting is an affirmative defense, not proper on a motion to dismiss/demurrer. "There is no statutory authority for using a motion to dismiss as a method to challenge opposing pleadings. Under the Code, dismissal motions are expressly authorized only on specified grounds." (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-D, § 7:370.) Demurrers are similarly limited to the grounds specified in Code fo Civ. Proc. 430.10. Such an argument should be raised as an affirmative defense in D's answer, provided for in Code of Civ. Proc. § 431.30(g). Fraud is an affirmative defense to be raised in a contract action. (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 6-C, § 6:439.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, N.A. VS. BRADLEY D. CARRIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 23, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Denied. There is a mandatory forum selection clause in Plaintiff and Defendant's loan agreement by which Plaintiff agreed on request to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of San Francisco County. "Forum selection clauses are valid and may be given effect." (Miller-Leigh v. Henson (2007) 152 Cal.App.1143, 1149.) Thus, this court has personal jurisdiction over Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)
